nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin object with a static-init System.loadLibrary and a wall of `external fun` JNI declarations; serializes TOC/search results as ASCII-separated strings.

nodes.name: ReflowCss.kt
nodes.summary: Pure translator from the engine-neutral domain ReflowConfig into crengine-ng inputs: engine properties (real PROP_* keys) via toProperties and a user CSS snippet (text-align, chapter page breaks) via toUserCss.
nodes.languageNotes: Side-effect-free Kotlin object mapping a domain config to engine PROP_* constants and hyphenation pattern-dictionary selection — designed for trivial TDD.

nodes.name: toProperties
nodes.summary: Builds the crengine-ng engine-property map for a ReflowConfig: interline space, page margins, font face/weight, and hyphenation settings.

nodes.name: toUserCss
nodes.summary: Produces the user stylesheet snippet for styles crengine-ng cannot express as properties: body text-align and a DocFragment page-break that starts chapters on a new page.

nodes.name: ReflowCssTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for ReflowCss verifying that line height, margins, font face/weight, and hyphenation map to the real crengine-ng PROP_* keys, and that text-align and chapter breaks are emitted as user CSS rather than properties.

nodes.name: settings.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle settings script declaring plugin/dependency repositories (incl. Ghostscript and the Onyx Boox Maven repo) and including all 14 project modules (domain, ui-api, sources, render engines, plugin host/sdk, app).
nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin DSL settings file; defines the multi-module structure that enforces the project's architectural seams.

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle build script for the source-api module (Naht-A contract): pure Kotlin JVM library that re-exports :domain via api() and adds coroutines plus test dependencies.

nodes.name: CollectionSyncSource.kt
nodes.summary: Optional Naht-A capability interface for sources that can write user collections server-side (list/create/update/delete with replace semantics), gated by a canWriteCollections check.

nodes.name: MediaSource.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the Naht-A source contract: the base MediaSource interface plus BrowsableSource (browse/search/read/cover/download), SyncingSource (progress sync), and ContainerSource, with supporting value types (PageRef, PagedResult, SourceFilter, SourceContainer).
nodes.languageNotes: Source-agnostic suspend interfaces with nullable/empty return contracts so partial sources can opt out; the central abstraction the whole app reads through.

nodes.name: PageRefs.kt
nodes.summary: Single pure helper buildPageRefs that deterministically constructs 1-based PageRefs from a page count without any network call, source-neutral.

nodes.name: SourceId.kt
nodes.summary: Generates globally unique, deterministic 64-bit source IDs from name/kind/config via SHA-256 (Mihon-style), with ID 0 reserved for the local source.

nodes.name: of
nodes.summary: Hashes name/kind/config with SHA-256 and folds the first 8 bytes into a non-negative 64-bit ID, clearing the sign bit so IDs are always >= 0.

nodes.name: SourceManager.kt
nodes.summary: Reactive registry of active sources (id -> MediaSource) backed by a StateFlow; the single source-agnostic access point for library/reader/sync, with getOrStub guaranteeing a missing backend never breaks the library.

nodes.name: SourceManager
nodes.summary: Holds a MutableStateFlow map of registered sources and exposes register/unregister/get/getOrStub plus an observable sources flow, with no CoroutineScope kept inside the domain module.

nodes.name: StubSource.kt
nodes.summary: Placeholder MediaSource that holds title/ID for library entries whose real source is unavailable (remote server down, plugin uninstalled), so the library never breaks; browsing/sync deliberately fail.

nodes.name: FoundationE2ETest.kt
nodes.summary: End-to-end wiring test of the domain foundation: deterministic source ID -> SourceManager registry -> ResolveViewerType over a shelf -> ResolveProgressConflict, proving the domain building blocks compose as a pipeline.

nodes.name: BrowsableSourceContractTest.kt
nodes.summary: Contract test using a FakeBrowsable implementation to verify the BrowsableSource interface methods downloadFile, seriesIdOf, and coverBytes are satisfiable and behave through the interface.

nodes.name: PageRefsTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for buildPageRefs verifying 1-based page numbers with 0-based indices and an empty result for a zero page count.

nodes.name: SourceIdTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for SourceId verifying ID stability for equal inputs, distinctness for different configs, non-negativity, and the reserved local ID 0.

nodes.name: SourceManagerTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for SourceManager covering get for registered/unknown sources, getOrStub returning a StubSource, and the sources StateFlow emitting after registration (verified with Turbine).

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle build script for the source-komga module: Kotlin JVM library with serialization, depending on :domain and :source-api plus Retrofit/OkHttp/kotlinx.serialization and MockWebServer for tests.

nodes.name: ApiKeyInterceptor.kt
nodes.summary: OkHttp interceptor that attaches the Komga API key as an X-API-Key header on every request.

nodes.name: BasicAuthInterceptor.kt
nodes.summary: OkHttp interceptor that attaches HTTP Basic auth credentials as an Authorization header on every request.

nodes.name: CollectionDtos.kt
nodes.summary: kotlinx.serialization DTOs for Komga collections and read-lists (read, create, update) plus a KomgaUserDto exposing roles for the write capability.

nodes.name: KomgaDtos.kt
nodes.summary: kotlinx.serialization DTOs for the core Komga REST shapes: the Spring Page envelope, series/book/metadata/media, read progress, pages, and libraries.

nodes.name: KomgaApi.kt
nodes.summary: Retrofit interface declaring the Komga REST v1 (and v2 users/me) endpoints for series, books, pages, thumbnails, read-progress, collections, and read-lists.
nodes.languageNotes: Retrofit suspend functions with @GET/@POST/@PATCH/@DELETE and @Streaming for binary page/file/thumbnail bytes; paths are relative to the .../api/v1/ base URL.

nodes.name: KomgaFormat.kt
nodes.summary: Pure mapping from a Komga mediaType string to the internal BookFormat enum (CBR/PDF/EPUB/CBZ) via prefix matching, defaulting unknown types to CBZ.

nodes.name: mediaTypeToFormat
nodes.summary: Prefix-matches a Komga mediaType string against known content types and returns the corresponding BookFormat, falling back to CBZ.

nodes.name: KomgaMapper.kt
nodes.summary: Pure mapper translating Komga DTOs into domain models (Series, Book, PageRef, ReadProgress, RemoteCollection), including reading-direction normalization, cover/page URL construction, and robust ISO-to-UTC timestamp parsing.

nodes.name: KomgaMapper
nodes.summary: Stateless mapper (holding sourceId and baseUrl) with pure toSeries/toBook/toPageRefs/toReadProgress/toRemoteCollection methods and a UTC-safe ISO timestamp parser.

nodes.name: parseIsoUtcMillis
nodes.summary: Parses a Komga timestamp to UTC epoch millis, trying Instant, OffsetDateTime, then zoneless LocalDateTime-as-UTC, returning 0 for blank/unparseable input.

nodes.name: KomgaSource.kt
nodes.summary: Concrete Komga backend behind the domain seams, implementing BrowsableSource/SyncingSource/ContainerSource/CollectionSyncSource over Retrofit, plus the KomgaSourceFactory that assembles the Retrofit client with API-key or Basic-auth interceptors.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements four source-api interfaces over a KomgaApi; streams whole-file downloads with progress callbacks and routes collection vs read-list endpoints by CollectionKind.

nodes.name: KomgaSource
nodes.summary: Komga MediaSource implementation covering browse/search/series detail/books/pages/cover/download, progress push-pull and read marking, and collection/read-list sync, delegating HTTP to KomgaApi and translation to KomgaMapper.

nodes.name: downloadFile
nodes.summary: Streams a whole book file from Komga in 64KB chunks into a byte buffer, reporting running (read, total) progress, with total -1 when no Content-Length is available.

nodes.name: ApiKeyInterceptorTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit test verifying the Komga API-key OkHttp interceptor injects the X-API-Key header on outgoing requests.

nodes.name: BasicAuthInterceptorTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit test verifying the basic-auth OkHttp interceptor adds the correct Authorization header for Komga requests.

nodes.name: KomgaCollectionSyncTest.kt
nodes.summary: MockWebServer test exercising the Komga source's collection sync behavior (listing, members, push/pull of collections).

nodes.name: KomgaDownloadTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit test that the Komga source downloads a book file and reports byte progress through the BrowsableSource seam.

nodes.name: KomgaE2ETest.kt
nodes.summary: End-to-end test driving the Komga source against a live local test-Komga instance to verify the full browse/read pipeline.

nodes.name: KomgaFormatTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit test for the Komga media-type to BookFormat mapping helper covering CBZ/CBR/PDF/EPUB and fallbacks.

nodes.name: KomgaMapperCollectionTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit test for the Komga collection-DTO mapper, asserting correct mapping of remote collections to the domain model including empty cases.

nodes.name: KomgaMapperTest.kt
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit test for the Komga DTO-to-domain mapper covering series, books, metadata, progress and null/empty fallbacks.

nodes.name: KomgaSourceTest.kt
nodes.summary: MockWebServer contract test for KomgaSource covering browse, search, books, pages, openPage, progress push/pull and library listing.

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle build script for the source-opds module: pure-JVM Kotlin library depending on domain, source-api, coroutines and OkHttp.
nodes.languageNotes: Kotlin DSL build script using the version catalog (libs.*) and a JVM toolchain of 21.

nodes.name: OpdsFeedParser.kt
nodes.summary: Namespace-aware DOM parser for OPDS Atom feeds with XXE hardening, extracting entries with cover and acquisition links.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the JVM built-in DocumentBuilderFactory with tolerant per-feature XXE hardening for Android Harmony parser compatibility.

nodes.name: OpdsFormat.kt
nodes.summary: Maps an OPDS acquisition-link MIME type to the domain BookFormat enum, defaulting unknown types to CBZ.

nodes.name: OpdsModels.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the OpdsEntry data class representing a single Atom entry parsed from an OPDS feed.

nodes.name: OpdsSource.kt
nodes.summary: Concrete BrowsableSource implementation backing an OPDS Atom catalog over OkHttp, mapping entries to Series/Book and reading via file download.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the source-agnostic seam (BrowsableSource); reading goes through downloadFile rather than page streaming, with optional basic-auth.

nodes.name: OpdsE2ETest.kt
nodes.summary: End-to-end test driving OpdsSource against a live OPDS endpoint to verify feed browsing and book download.

nodes.name: OpdsFeedParserTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit test for OpdsFeedParser verifying entry extraction, cover/acquisition link resolution and XXE-hardened parsing.

nodes.name: OpdsFormatTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit test for the OPDS MIME-type to BookFormat mapping helper including the CBZ fallback for unknown types.

nodes.name: OpdsSourceTest.kt
nodes.summary: MockWebServer contract test for OpdsSource covering browse, search, books, download, cover loading and series resolution.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose stack running three Komga instances plus a Kavita server with read-only fixture content for source integration tests.
nodes.languageNotes: Mounts fixture content read-only and uses per-instance config volumes to cache scan results; mirror instance komga-c replicates komga-a.

nodes.name: komga-a
nodes.summary: Primary Komga test instance on port 25701 serving the Manga and Novels-A libraries from read-only fixtures.

nodes.name: komga-b
nodes.summary: Second Komga test instance on port 25702 serving the disjoint Webtoon and Novels-B libraries.

nodes.name: komga-c
nodes.summary: Mirror Komga instance on port 25703 replicating komga-a's libraries to exercise the multi-equal-server scenario.

nodes.name: kavita
nodes.summary: Kavita server on port 25710 reusing the CC0 manga fixtures to back the Kavita source-plugin end-to-end test.

nodes.name: down.sh
nodes.summary: Shell helper that stops the CI fixture containers, optionally wiping config volumes and seed markers with the -v flag.

nodes.name: freeze-from-nas.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that rsync-freezes a curated copyrighted NAS subset into runner-local Tier-2 fixtures that must never be committed.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses set -euo pipefail strict mode and an idempotent rsync --delete loop over a curated selection array.

nodes.name: err
nodes.summary: Convenience logger that writes prefixed diagnostic messages to STDERR.

nodes.name: gen-fixtures.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic generator for CC0 test fixtures, producing bit-stable CBZ comics and EPUB novels for the Komga integration test libraries.
nodes.languageNotes: Pins ZIP timestamps and uses Pillow-drawn color pages to keep committed Tier-1 content bit-deterministic across regenerations.

nodes.name: _page
nodes.summary: Draws a single labeled color page with a border via Pillow and returns its PNG bytes.

nodes.name: _write_zip
nodes.summary: Writes entries to a deterministic ZIP archive with fixed timestamps, optionally storing an uncompressed mimetype first for EPUBs.

nodes.name: make_cbz
nodes.summary: Builds a CBZ comic volume with the given page count and per-page color labels.

nodes.name: make_epub
nodes.summary: Assembles a minimal valid EPUB3 with container, OPF package and the requested number of XHTML chapters.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that generates the full set of manga, webtoon and novel fixtures into the content directory.

nodes.name: manifest.json
nodes.summary: Declares the expected Tier-1 libraries, series and book counts plus the three Komga instance topology used to assert deterministic fixtures.

nodes.name: PROVENANCE.md
nodes.summary: Data-provenance documentation for the CI fixtures, recording license, volume and risk for committed CC0 Tier-1 content and the runner-local copyrighted Tier-2 subset.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Overview of the CI fixture backends documenting quick-start commands, the three-Komga topology, content tiers, determinism and CI usage.

nodes.name: seed-kavita.sh
nodes.summary: CI fixture script that waits for a Kavita server to come up, authenticates, and seeds it with test libraries for plugin E2E tests.

nodes.name: wait_up
nodes.summary: Polls the Kavita server until it responds ready, aborting after a timeout.

nodes.name: auth
nodes.summary: Authenticates against the Kavita API and obtains the credentials needed to seed fixtures.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the Kavita seeding flow: wait, authenticate, and create the test data.

nodes.name: seed.sh
nodes.summary: CI fixture script that claims the Komga admin account, mints an API key, creates a library, and waits for series to be scanned for E2E tests.

nodes.name: claim_admin
nodes.summary: Claims the initial Komga admin account on a fresh instance.

nodes.name: make_api_key
nodes.summary: Creates and returns a Komga API key used by the test harness to authenticate requests.

nodes.name: create_library
nodes.summary: Creates a Komga library pointing at the seeded sample comics directory.

nodes.name: wait_series_count
nodes.summary: Polls Komga until the expected number of series has been scanned into the library.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the Komga seeding flow: wait, claim admin, mint key, create library, wait for scan.

nodes.name: up.sh
nodes.summary: CI fixture script that brings up the test backend containers (Komga/Kavita) and waits until each is ready and serving content.

nodes.name: wait_ready
nodes.summary: Polls a service endpoint until it reports healthy, aborting after a timeout.

nodes.name: verify.sh
nodes.summary: CI fixture script that asserts the seeded test instances expose the expected series counts and content.

nodes.name: assert_instance
nodes.summary: Asserts a seeded test instance returns the expected series and fails the script otherwise.

nodes.name: emulator-down.sh
nodes.summary: CI helper that tears down the running Android emulator after instrumentation tests complete.

nodes.name: emulator-up.sh
nodes.summary: CI helper that boots the Android emulator and waits for it to reach a usable state before running instrumentation tests.

nodes.name: LICENSE-SCAN-NG.md
nodes.summary: Phase-0 license audit of the crengine-ng EPUB engine candidate, concluding GREEN (GPL-2.0-or-later, AGPL-compatible) based on repo-level and per-file header evidence.

nodes.name: LICENSE-SCAN.md
nodes.summary: Phase-0 license audit of the original crengine candidate, concluding RED (GPL-2.0-only, incompatible with the project's AGPL) so the feature was not built.

nodes.name: scan-license.sh
nodes.summary: Phase-0 gate script that classifies the GPL license of each crengine source file as or-later, only, or unknown to verify AGPL compatibility.
nodes.languageNotes: Collapses multi-line license headers to a single line so 'version 2 ... or any later version' blurbs match as one regex hit.

nodes.name: app_smoke.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end smoke test that installs and launches the app on a device/emulator and checks it starts without crashing.

nodes.name: generate.mjs
nodes.summary: Icon code generator that reads Lucide SVGs, converts them to path data, and emits the LucideIcons.kt registry consumed by the ui-api module.
nodes.languageNotes: ESM script; the generated Kotlin object is written verbatim to ui-api so call sites never edit it by hand.

nodes.name: icon-set.mjs
nodes.summary: Declares the ICONS and FILLED maps (Lucide kebab name to Kotlin property) that drive icon code generation.

nodes.name: svg-to-pathdata.mjs
nodes.summary: Pure converter that turns SVG primitive elements (line, polyline, rect, circle, ellipse, path) into a single normalized SVG path-data string.

nodes.name: polyToPath
nodes.summary: Converts an SVG polyline/polygon's point list into a path-data move/line sequence, optionally closing the shape.

nodes.name: rectToPath
nodes.summary: Converts an SVG rect (including rounded corners) into equivalent path-data.

nodes.name: svgToPathData
nodes.summary: Top-level converter that scans an SVG string, dispatches each primitive element to its converter, and joins the results into one path-data string.

nodes.name: svg-to-pathdata.test.mjs
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the SVG-to-path-data converter, covering each primitive shape conversion.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node package manifest for the icon generator tool, declaring the lucide-static and svgpath dependencies and the generate script.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the icon generator: Lucide provenance/license, how to regenerate, how to change stroke width, and the tool's architecture.

nodes.name: make_sample_comics.py
nodes.summary: Spike script that generates synthetic CBZ sample comics (drawn pages packed into zip archives) for seeding a local Komga test instance.

nodes.name: _page
nodes.summary: Draws a single synthetic comic page image with panels and labels using Pillow.

nodes.name: make_cbz
nodes.summary: Packs a sequence of generated page images into a CBZ (zip) archive on disk.

nodes.name: make_fixtures.py
nodes.summary: Spike script that builds CBZ, PDF, and EPUB test fixtures with synthetic pages for exercising the MuPDF render path.

nodes.name: make_epub
nodes.summary: Assembles a minimal valid EPUB archive (container, OPF, content) for render testing.

nodes.name: render_spike.py
nodes.summary: MuPDF render spike that builds CBZ/PDF/EPUB fixtures, renders their first page via mutool, and verifies the output PNG is non-blank.

nodes.name: make_epub
nodes.summary: Builds a complete minimal EPUB (mimetype, container, OPF, NCX, XHTML content) for the render spike.

nodes.name: check_rendered
nodes.summary: Verifies a rendered PNG exists, is non-empty, and contains non-white pixels (i.e. actually rendered content).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the spike: checks mutool, builds each fixture format, renders and validates its first page, and reports results.

nodes.name: build.gradle.kts
nodes.summary: Gradle build script for the ui-api Android library module that holds the in-tree UI contracts (region slots, shell pack, theme pack, icon pack) and decoupled built-ins.
nodes.languageNotes: Enforces the domain -> ui-api -> app DAG via api(project(":domain")); not yet ABI-frozen pending the L1/L2 pack loader.

nodes.name: AppIcons.kt
nodes.summary: Central icon registry (single source of truth) exposing semantically named ImageVector properties that delegate through ActiveIconPack/IconKey to the active icon pack.
nodes.languageNotes: Each property is a get() delegate to ActiveIconPack.resolve(IconKey.X), enabling app-wide glyph swap without touching call sites.

nodes.name: AppIcons
nodes.summary: Object registry of semantic icon properties; the only icon surface the UI is allowed to reference, each delegating to the active icon pack.

nodes.name: IconPack.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the IconKey enum, the IconPack functional interface, the DefaultIconPack Lucide mapping, and the process-global ActiveIconPack holder for app-wide swappable icons.
nodes.languageNotes: ActiveIconPack is deliberately process-global (@Volatile var) rather than a CompositionLocal, since AppIcons is read outside composition; a partial pack returning null falls back to DefaultIconPack.

nodes.name: DefaultIconPack
nodes.summary: Bundled default icon pack mapping every IconKey to its Lucide glyph; the single Glyph-to-meaning map covering all keys.

nodes.name: ActiveIconPack
nodes.summary: App-wide swappable icon-pack holder whose resolve() falls back per-key to DefaultIconPack so a partial pack never leaves a hole.

nodes.name: LucideIcons.kt
nodes.summary: Generated registry of Lucide glyphs as ImageVectors with an E-Ink-tuned stroke; produced by tools/icons/generate.mjs and not hand-edited.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated; central STROKE constant (2.5f) tunes E-Ink stroke width without regenerating, and lazy delegates build each ImageVector on first use.

nodes.name: LucideIcons
nodes.summary: Object holding the generated Lucide ImageVectors and the lucide/lucideFilled builders that apply the E-Ink stroke.

nodes.name: AppShellState.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the AppShellState capability surface and ShellDestination pieces: the named, host-built home-skeleton surface that a ShellPack arranges but never rebuilds.
nodes.languageNotes: Surface is a set of named renderable pieces (not an opaque blob), which is the precondition that lets the in-tree Compose shell evolve into a declarative descriptor-driven one.

nodes.name: ShellDescriptor.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the ShellNavStyle vocabulary, the Compose-free ShellDescriptor data descriptor, and the pure descriptorFor(formFactor) resolution driving the declarative shell.
nodes.languageNotes: Compose-free, unit-testable; descriptorFor maps EXPANDED to BOTTOM_BAR and COMPACT to DRAWER, carrying the built-in choice as serializable data for the L2 external pack.

nodes.name: ShellPack.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the Shell-Pack seam contract: the ShellPack interface that arranges AppShellState pieces into a whole home skeleton, plus pure form-factor resolution from screen width with an optional user override.
nodes.languageNotes: Compose-free pure functions (formFactorFor/resolveFormFactor) kept separate from the @Composable ShellPack.Render so the resolution logic stays unit-testable.

nodes.name: DetailScaffoldState.kt
nodes.summary: Capability surface for the full-screen detail scaffold region: title, back, header actions, optional header search, optional snackbar host and the host-built body that a DetailSlot pack frames.

nodes.name: DialogState.kt
nodes.summary: Capability surface for the dialog region, mirroring the EinkModal parameters (title, confirm/dismiss callbacks and labels, optional header action, body) that a DialogSlot pack arranges.

nodes.name: HomeHeaderState.kt
nodes.summary: Capability surface of the home header (status cluster, search, generic filter slot, menu overlay, tab actions) that a HomeHeaderSlot pack rearranges without reimplementing host logic.

nodes.name: ReaderOverlayState.kt
nodes.summary: Capability surface of the togglable reader chrome menu bar (title, navigation/shortcut callbacks, reader-specific actions); deliberately carries no visibility flag since chromeVisible and the E-Ink scrim are host-enforced.

nodes.name: ReaderScaffoldState.kt
nodes.summary: Capability surface of the whole reader chrome scaffold (background, declarative tap zones, hints, chrome bar, footer, persistent bars, content); deliberately excludes the Viewer (seam B) carrying only derived chromeVisible/onToggleChrome.

nodes.name: ReaderTapZones.kt
nodes.summary: Declarative tap-zone descriptor for the reader region where geometry belongs to the host and the screen supplies only per-zone actions, plus a pure dispatch routing a normalized x-fraction to left/center/right thirds.
nodes.languageNotes: Sealed interface with a pure extension-function dispatch keeps the thirds geometry in one unit-testable place, replacing the prior opaque tapModifier.

nodes.name: SettingsSection.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the SettingsSectionId enum and the SettingsSection data model (metadata, search terms, scrollable flag, host-built content) that compose the settings screen.

nodes.name: SettingsState.kt
nodes.summary: Minimal capability surface of the settings region: the host-built SettingsSections plus the search query that a SettingsSlot pack arranges while owning navigation state itself.

nodes.name: TileState.kt
nodes.summary: Capability surface of the tiles region: the Series work, its local status and navigation callbacks; cover loading and the E-Ink filter stay host-enforced and outside the surface.

nodes.name: UiSlots.kt
nodes.summary: Core of the UI-slot seam: declares all eight chrome region slot type-aliases (header, homeHeader, dialog, settings, tiles, overlay, detail, readerChrome), the UiSlotPack/ResolvedSlots contracts, and the pure UiSlots.resolve fallback logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of @Composable function type-aliases as slot contracts; the pure resolve passes references through unchanged so referential identity is the unit-test invariant.

nodes.name: AuroraPack.kt
nodes.summary: Defines AuroraPack, the modern-mobile LCD-class UiPack (Slate/Deeper-Grey palette with a Cobalt accent, soft large radii, shadow depth, motion allowed) selected in smartphone mode.

nodes.name: DepthSurface.kt
nodes.summary: Provides the depthSurface Composable Modifier that renders depth device-class correctly: shadow elevation on LCD versus a hairline border on E-Ink, driven by DesignTokens.usesShadows.

nodes.name: DesignTokens.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the per-device-class skin tokens (accent, shadow usage, elevation, corner radius) and the pure designTokensFor mapping from DisplayBehavior across mono/Kaleido/LCD classes.
nodes.languageNotes: Two orthogonal DisplayBehavior axes (motion, accent) collapse via a when-expression; the nonsensical (motion, !accent) combo deliberately falls back to the mono branch.

nodes.name: EinkTokens.kt
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for the Onyx E-Ink layout measurements (hairline/strong border widths, tile radius, paddings, gaps, nav icon size) so no magic dp values land in UI code.

nodes.name: UiPack.kt
nodes.summary: Defines the UiPack interface (full look of a device class: color scheme, design tokens, shapes, typography), its three built-in implementations (mono E-Ink, Kaleido, LCD) and the pure packFor device-class selector.
nodes.languageNotes: Built-in packs are anonymous object expressions implementing the UiPack interface; Material3 light/dark color schemes are defined as private vals shared across packs.

nodes.name: UiPackRegistry.kt
nodes.summary: Registry object enumerating the built-in UiPacks and resolving them by id or device behavior; the in-tree hook point where a future user/community-supplied pack would register.

nodes.name: IconPackTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the process-global icon-pack seam (ActiveIconPack.resolve): default-pack completeness over every IconKey, partial-pack fallback to default, and glyph override.

nodes.name: ShellDescriptorTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the shell descriptor resolution (descriptorFor) mapping form factor to the ShellNavStyle nav-arrangement vocabulary (EXPANDED to bottom bar, COMPACT to drawer).

nodes.name: ShellFormFactorTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the pure shell form-factor resolution: formFactorFor (width-based compact/expanded split at 600dp) and resolveFormFactor (with ShellLayoutMode user override).

nodes.name: ReaderTapZonesTest.kt
nodes.summary: Pure geometry tests of the declarative tap-zone dispatch (HorizontalThirds.dispatch) verifying left/center/right routing and the exact thirds boundary behavior, without Compose.

nodes.name: SlotFallbackTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the UI-slot resolution logic (UiSlots.resolve) covering all eight regions: a missing slot falls back to the default and a supplied slot overrides it, asserted by referential identity.

nodes.name: AuroraPackTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for AuroraPack: id, the dark/light Slate-grey + Cobalt color schemes, the LCD-class Cobalt design tokens with shadows, and the soft 16dp medium shape.

nodes.name: DesignTokensTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for designTokensFor verifying the mono/Kaleido/LCD mappings against named accent constants, axis independence, and the safe mono fallback for the nonsensical (motion, !accent) combo.

nodes.name: UiPackTest.kt
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the UiPack selection and registry: packFor device-class mapping, mono fallback, per-pack design tokens and color schemes, and UiPackRegistry id/behavior resolution and enumeration.

nodes.name: UiSlots
nodes.summary: Resolver object for the slot seam: its pure resolve(pack, defaults) returns ResolvedSlots picking each region's pack slot or the host default, keeping referential identity intact for testing.

nodes.name: UiSlotPack
nodes.summary: A slot pack with one optional slot per chrome region (header, homeHeader, dialog, settings, tiles, overlay, detail, readerChrome); null means do not override that region. New regions are added additively.

nodes.name: ResolvedSlots
nodes.summary: Resolved slots where every region is guaranteed non-null (defaults filled in where the pack supplied nothing), consumed app-wide via the LocalResolvedSlots CompositionLocal.

nodes.name: header
nodes.summary: Backward-compatible header extension on ResolvedSlots that builds a search-less HeaderState and invokes headerSlot, keeping existing current.header(title, onBack){actions} call sites unchanged.

nodes.name: UiPack
nodes.summary: Interface for the full look of a device class: id, color scheme (light/dark), design tokens, shapes and typography. The first real contract of the modular UI theme seam, implemented by the built-in and future community packs.

nodes.name: packFor
nodes.summary: Pure selector mapping a DisplayBehavior to a built-in UiPack across the two orthogonal axes: mono E-Ink when no accent, Kaleido when no motion, Aurora otherwise; the nonsensical combo falls safely onto mono.

nodes.name: DesignTokens
nodes.summary: Immutable data class of skin tokens that vary by device class: accent, onAccent, usesShadows, cardElevation and cornerRadius. The single source of truth derived from DisplayBehavior.

nodes.name: designTokensFor
nodes.summary: Pure mapping from a DisplayBehavior to DesignTokens across the three device classes, branching on the two orthogonal axes with a safe mono fallback for the nonsensical (motion, !accent) combination.

nodes.name: AuroraPack
nodes.summary: The Aurora UiPack implementation: a modern-mobile LCD-class look with Cobalt accent, soft 16dp radii, shadow depth and the AuroraDark/AuroraLight color schemes and Aurora typography.

nodes.name: UiPackRegistry
nodes.summary: Registry object that enumerates the built-in UiPacks (all), resolves one by stable id (byId), and selects the active pack for a device behavior (forBehavior delegating to packFor).

nodes.name: depthSurface
nodes.summary: Composable Modifier extension that renders depth correctly per device class: shadow + clip + background on LCD, or clip + background + hairline border on E-Ink, driven by DesignTokens.usesShadows.

nodes.name: ShellPack
nodes.summary: Interface for a shell pack that arranges the AppShellState pieces into a complete home skeleton via a Composable Render; built-ins are Compose while external declarative packs reuse the same surface.

nodes.name: resolveFormFactor
nodes.summary: Pure resolution of the effective shell form factor honoring the ShellLayoutMode user override: AUTO derives from width via formFactorFor, COMPACT/EXPANDED force the choice.

nodes.name: ReaderTapZones
nodes.summary: Sealed interface describing the reader's declarative tap zones; the only geometry today is HorizontalThirds carrying a left/center/right action per zone, host-owned geometry, screen-owned actions.

nodes.name: dispatch
nodes.summary: Pure HorizontalThirds extension routing a normalized x-fraction to the matching zone action: below 1/3 left, above 2/3 right, otherwise center (boundaries themselves fall on center).

nodes.name: HomeHeaderState
nodes.summary: Capability surface of the home header bundling status, search, an optional generic filter slot, a menu overlay and tab actions; the host builds it and the pack only arranges these capabilities.

nodes.name: ReaderScaffoldState
nodes.summary: Capability surface of the whole reader chrome scaffold (background, declarative tap zones, hints, chrome bar callbacks, footer, persistent bars, content) carrying derived chromeVisible/onToggleChrome but never the Viewer seam.

layers.name: Domain Core
layers.description: Pure-Kotlin heart of the app: data models, use-cases, ViewerType resolution, and the repository/render/E-Ink interface contracts that depend on neither Android nor any concrete source.

layers.name: Seam A — Sources
layers.description: The source-agnostic backend seam: the MediaSource/BrowsableSource contract (source-api) plus the Komga REST and OPDS adapter implementations behind it.

layers.name: Seam B — Render & Device
layers.description: The render/device seam: MuPDF page rendering (render-core), crengine EPUB reflow (render-crengine), and the Onyx E-Ink controller (eink-onyx) behind the Document/EinkController interfaces.

layers.name: Guided-View Panel Detection
layers.description: Pure-Kotlin XY-cut panel detection that powers the guided comic reading mode, kept engine- and UI-independent.

layers.name: Plugin System
layers.description: Runtime extensibility stack: the plugin ABI contract (plugin-api), the TOFU-pinning APK loader (plugin-host), and the shaded single-jar SDK (plugin-sdk) that external plugins compile against.

layers.name: UI Modularity Contracts
layers.description: The UI-pack seam: slot/shell/theme/icon capability contracts plus the decoupled built-in theme packs and icon stack, sitting between domain and the app shell (domain → ui-api → app).

layers.name: Data & Persistence
layers.description: Room database implementations, the offline-first sync queue, collection sync, and the download manager — the persistence layer below the UI.

layers.name: App Shell
layers.description: The imperative shell: Compose UI, ViewModels, Hilt DI wiring, the reader host, and the coupled default UI packs/renderers that wire every seam together.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Unit, integration, and instrumented Android tests across all modules — domain/mapper unit tests, source contract tests, and the CI fixture-driven E2E suites under androidTest.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation: design specs, implementation plans, UI-pack guides under docs/, plus the root project guide (CLAUDE.md) and license NOTICE.

layers.name: Project Tooling & Build
layers.description: Build and developer tooling: Gradle build scripts and version catalog, the wrapper, icon/CI tooling under tools/, and the GitLab CI pipeline with its Docker fixture services.

tour.title: Project Overview & Invariants
tour.description: Start here. CLAUDE.md is the canonical project guide: it states the five non-negotiable invariants and, above all, the two-seam design that carries the whole app — Seam A (sources) and Seam B (render/E-Ink). The design spec goes deeper, laying out the full architecture, the three reading modes, and the source/device-agnostic goal. Read these two to understand WHY every later module is shaped the way it is: maximum flexibility behind stable seams.

tour.title: The Pure Domain Core
tour.description: Everything above the seams is source- and device-agnostic, and it all speaks the vocabulary defined here. The domain module is pure Kotlin with no Android, network, or source dependency. Series and Book are the neutral domain models that every source fills in as best it can; the use-cases operate only on these. This is the foundational layer — read it first because every other layer depends on its types and contracts.

tour.title: Viewer Type Resolution
tour.description: Building on the Series/Book models from Step 2, the reader mode is chosen deterministically — never by fragile content sniffing. ViewerType enumerates the modes (paged comic, webtoon, guided comic, novel reflow); ResolveViewerType applies the fixed priority rule (Series.contentTypeOverride takes precedence over the shelf's content type). This is a pure use-case, so it is unit-tested in isolation with no Android setup.

tour.title: Seam A — The MediaSource Contract
tour.description: This is the most depended-upon file in the codebase (highest fan-in) and the heart of source-agnosticism. MediaSource is the base backend contract; BrowsableSource adds browse/search/read/cover/download, SyncingSource adds progress sync. SourceManager is the registry that holds every active connection by a stable, deterministic id. Crucially, the domain and UI know ONLY these interfaces — never a concrete server. A new backend is a new implementation behind this contract, not a core rewrite.

tour.title: Concrete Sources — Komga & OPDS
tour.description: These two modules prove the seam is real: each implements BrowsableSource against a different backend (Komga's REST API, the OPDS feed protocol) and shares no code with the other. They live in their own modules that depend only on domain — never on the UI, never on each other. KomgaSource has high fan-out because it maps a rich API; OpdsSource fills in only what the feed can provide and returns null for the rest. This is the source-extensibility recipe in action.

tour.title: Source-Agnostic Wiring
tour.description: A clean interface is worthless if the app talks past it to a concrete source — a mistake this project actually made and then fixed. ActiveSource is the agnostic boundary every ViewModel injects: it resolves a connection as a BrowsableSource by sourceId, never exposing a Komga type. SourceRegistration is the ONLY wiring class allowed to name concrete sources, building the registry from server config. The litmus test for any feature: does it still work if the active source is OPDS, a Stub, or a plugin — without changing a line?

tour.title: Seam B — Render & Device
tour.description: The second load-bearing seam isolates how pixels reach the screen. Document/DocumentFactory is the engine-neutral render contract; MupdfDocument backs it with the MuPDF C++/JNI engine (cbz/cbr/pdf and EPUB into a Bitmap), while CrengineDocumentFactory plugs in a separate reflow engine behind ReflowableDocument. EinkController is the device seam: OnyxEinkController drives the Boox refresh hardware (HW-gated), and NoOpEinkController is the fallback so development never crashes on non-Boox hardware.
tour.languageLesson: JNI bridges Kotlin to native C++ render engines (MuPDF, crengine). The Kotlin side declares an engine-neutral interface (Document); each native engine is one implementation behind it, so swapping engines never touches the readers above.

tour.title: The Viewer Seam & Four Reading Modes
tour.description: Above the render engine sits the Viewer contract — a single Compose-state seam (chromeVisible flow, shared RefreshScheduler, toggleChrome/navigateTo) that every reader ViewModel implements. The shared ReaderScaffold is the common chrome (tap zones, overlay, refresh wiring) extracted ONCE so the four modes never drift — critical because inconsistent refresh logic causes visible E-Ink ghosting. ReaderRoute dispatches by ViewerType to the right reader screen. A fifth reader would implement Viewer, not start a parallel line.

tour.title: Guided-View Panel Detection
tour.description: The guided comic mode (one panel at a time) is powered by a pure-Kotlin module that depends on nothing — no engine, no UI. PanelDetector finds comic panels with a hybrid Otsu binarization plus edge-seeded gutter flood and connected-components pipeline, then orders them for reading. Keeping detection pure means it is fully unit-testable against a ground-truth dataset, and the reader engine simply consumes its output.

tour.title: Data & Offline-First Persistence
tour.description: AppDatabase is the central Room database (version 17) declaring every entity and DAO plus the full v1→v17 migration chain and a seed callback — note its high fan-out, since most data flows through it. Read progress is offline-first: it is written locally as dirty, then drained by a sync queue. SyncCoordinator is the single sync/discovery seam (app start, server change, manual reload), and CollectionSyncManager executes bidirectional collection sync underneath it. Every record carries a sourceId so uninstalling a source degrades gracefully.
tour.languageLesson: Room migrations must be ordered and self-consistent: each step applies incremental schema changes so the database evolves with the app. A column added without care can trigger a destructive wipe — migrations here are explicit and tested, not auto-generated.

tour.title: The Plugin System
tour.description: Phase 4's runtime extensibility hangs off Seam A. plugin-api is the pure-JVM ABI contract: SourcePlugin (metadata, configSchema, create -> BrowsableSource) and PluginCategory (source, color-preset, language, reader-preset, UI-pack) gated by a two-integer ABI version. plugin-host is the runtime loader — it discovers separate APKs via PackageManager, loads them with PathClassLoader, and gates instantiation on a TOFU certificate-SHA-256 pin. plugin-sdk is the single shaded jar that external plugins compile against.
tour.languageLesson: ABI versioning uses two integers (VERSION, MIN_SUPPORTED) rather than semver strings: a plugin outside the supported range is never instantiated. New capabilities are added as optional interfaces, keeping old plugins compatible without a version bump.

tour.title: Modular UI — The Three-Layer Seam
tour.description: The same seam philosophy is applied to presentation, so the community can swap looks without forking. UiPack carries the full look of a device class (color scheme, tokens, shapes) chosen by display behavior. AppShellState/ShellDescriptor is the form-factor layer — a host-built surface a shell pack arranges (bottom-bar vs. drawer) by screen size. UiSlots declares all eight swappable chrome regions, and IconPack swaps glyphs app-wide. Theme first, layout next, slots last — risk-ordered exactly like the plugin rollout.

tour.title: App Entry Point & DI Wiring
tour.description: Finally, see where it all comes together. MainActivity is the single Compose activity hosting the whole navigation graph (home, detail, reader, settings) and wiring theme, device behavior, UI packs, and hardware buttons. AppModule is the Hilt module that provides app-wide singletons: the SourceManager registry, the Coil image loader with source-seam fetchers, and the MuPDF/crengine render factories. This imperative shell is the ONE layer allowed to know about everything — every concrete type is assembled here and nowhere else.
tour.languageLesson: Hilt (Dagger) dependency injection centralizes object graph construction: the @Singleton module is the imperative shell where concrete implementations are bound to the interfaces the rest of the app depends on, keeping every other layer free of wiring details.
